004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.lang.reflect.*;
021
022import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
023import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
024
025/**
026 * Public low-level pull-parser cursor over a Juneau-formatted document.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * A StAX-like enum cursor that emits a stream of {@link TokenType} events without materializing
030 * the whole object graph.  This is a format-neutral surface; the same cursor methods work across
031 * every Juneau format that implements {@link TokenReadable} (the structural-token role).
032 *
033 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
034 * <p class='bjava'>
035 *    <jc>// Tokenize a JSON document without materializing it.</jc>
036 *    <jk>try</jk> (TokenReader <jv>r</jv> = Json.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.parseTokens(<js>"{\"a\":1,\"b\":[true,null]}"</js>)) {
037 *       <jk>while</jk> (<jv>r</jv>.next() != TokenType.<jsf>END_OF_STREAM</jsf>) {
038 *          <jk>switch</jk> (<jv>r</jv>.getCurrentToken()) {
039 *             <jk>case</jk> <jsf>FIELD_NAME</jsf>   -&gt; handleKey(<jv>r</jv>.getFieldName());
040 *             <jk>case</jk> <jsf>VALUE_STRING</jsf> -&gt; handleString(<jv>r</jv>.getString());
041 *             <jk>case</jk> <jsf>VALUE_NUMBER</jsf> -&gt; handleNumber(<jv>r</jv>.getNumber());
042 *             <jk>default</jk> -&gt; { <jc>// structural </jc>}
043 *          }
044 *       }
045 *    }
046 * </p>
047 *
048 * <h5 class='section'>Contract:</h5>
049 * <ul>
050 *    <li>The cursor is <b>purely structural</b>.  It bypasses object swaps and {@code @Schema}
051 *       annotations &mdash; that is the role of the POJO databind path on
052 *       {@link Parser Parser} and its bridges.
053 *    <li>For numeric values both the original lexeme and a parsed {@link Number} are available
054 *       (see {@link #getNumberLexeme()} and {@link #getNumber()}); precision-sensitive
055 *       consumers should prefer the lexeme.
056 *    <li>Concrete implementations are not required to be thread-safe.
057 * </ul>
058 *
059 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
060 *    <li>{@link TokenWriter} &mdash; the push-side counterpart.
061 *    <li>{@link TokenType} &mdash; the format-neutral token vocabulary.
062 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SerializersAndParsers">Serializers and Parsers</a>
063 * </ul>
064 */
065public interface TokenReader extends RecordReader {
066
067   /**
068    * Canonical message thrown as an {@link IllegalStateException} when {@link #read(Class)} is
069    * called while the cursor is not positioned at a value boundary.
070    *
071    * <p>
072    * Shared across all formats so the positioning/error contract is uniform (see the
073    * <c>read</c> contract); implementations throw <c><jk>new</jk> IllegalStateException(
074    * {@link #MSG_READ_NON_VALUE_STATE})</c> rather than inventing their own wording.
075    */
076   String MSG_READ_NON_VALUE_STATE =
077      "read called from a non-value cursor state.  The cursor must be positioned such that next() "
078      + "would emit START_OBJECT / START_ARRAY / VALUE_*; the current state would emit a structural "
079      + "separator, field name, end-container, or end-of-stream instead.";
080
081   /**
082    * Advances the cursor to the next token.
083    *
084    * @return The token type of the new current token, or {@link TokenType#END_OF_STREAM} when
085    *    the underlying input is exhausted.  Never returns {@link TokenType#NOT_AVAILABLE} &mdash;
086    *    that value is reserved for the initial pre-{@code next()} state.
087    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
088    * @throws ParseException If the input is malformed for the format.
089    */
090   TokenType next() throws IOException, ParseException;
091
092   /**
093    * Returns the current token type without advancing.
094    *
095    * @return The current token type.  Returns {@link TokenType#NOT_AVAILABLE} before the first
096    *    call to {@link #next()}, and {@link TokenType#END_OF_STREAM} once the stream is exhausted.
097    */
098   TokenType getCurrentToken();
099
100   /**
101    * Returns the current depth of the cursor.
102    *
103    * <p>
104    * Depth starts at <c>0</c> at the document root and increases by 1 for each
105    * {@link TokenType#START_OBJECT} / {@link TokenType#START_ARRAY} encountered (and decreases on
106    * the matching end tokens).
107    *
108    * @return The current depth.
109    */
110   int getDepth();
111
112   /**
113    * Returns the field name when the current token is {@link TokenType#FIELD_NAME}.
114    *
115    * @return The field name.
116    * @throws IllegalStateException If the current token is not {@link TokenType#FIELD_NAME}.
117    */
118   String getFieldName();
119
120   /**
121    * Returns the current scalar value as a string.
122    *
123    * <p>
124    * Defined for {@link TokenType#VALUE_STRING} (the natural value), {@link TokenType#FIELD_NAME}
125    * (returns the field name), {@link TokenType#VALUE_NUMBER} (returns the lexeme), and
126    * {@link TokenType#VALUE_BOOLEAN} (returns <js>"true"</js>/<js>"false"</js>).  For
127    * {@link TokenType#VALUE_NULL} returns <jk>null</jk>.
128    *
129    * @return The string view of the current value.
130    * @throws IllegalStateException If the current token has no string view.
131    */
132   String getString();
133
134   /**
135    * Returns the current numeric value as a parsed {@link Number}.
136    *
137    * <p>
138    * Implementations may parse lazily; the returned {@link Number} subtype is implementation- and
139    * format-defined (e.g. {@link Long} / {@link Double} / {@link java.math.BigDecimal}).  Use
140    * {@link #getNumberLexeme()} to recover the original textual form.
141    *
142    * @return The parsed numeric value.
143    * @throws IllegalStateException If the current token is not {@link TokenType#VALUE_NUMBER}.
144    * @throws ParseException If the lexeme cannot be parsed.
145    */
146   Number getNumber() throws ParseException;
147
148   /**
149    * Returns the original numeric lexeme for the current numeric token.
150    *
151    * <p>
152    * For text formats this is the exact substring that produced the number.  For binary formats
153    * the implementation returns a canonical text rendering of the native numeric token.
154    *
155    * @return The numeric lexeme.
156    * @throws IllegalStateException If the current token is not {@link TokenType#VALUE_NUMBER}.
157    */
158   String getNumberLexeme();
159
160   /**
161    * Returns the current boolean value.
162    *
163    * @return The boolean value.
164    * @throws IllegalStateException If the current token is not {@link TokenType#VALUE_BOOLEAN}.
165    */
166   boolean getBool();
167
168   /**
169    * Returns the current binary value.
170    *
171    * <p>
172    * Binary scalars come from binary formats only (cbor, msgpack, bson, ...).  Text formats
173    * deliver embedded binary content as base64-encoded {@link TokenType#VALUE_STRING}.
174    *
175    * @return The binary value.
176    * @throws IllegalStateException If the current token is not {@link TokenType#VALUE_BINARY}.
177    */
178   byte[] getBinary();
179
180   /**
181    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if {@link #read(Class)} is currently callable on this cursor &mdash;
182    * i.e. the cursor is positioned at a value boundary.
183    *
184    * <p>
185    * This is the natural loop predicate when streaming records:
186    * <p class='bjava'>
187    *    <jk>try</jk> (TokenReader <jv>r</jv> = Json.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.parseTokens(<jv>input</jv>)) {
188    *       <jv>r</jv>.next();  <jc>// consume the opening [ </jc>
189    *       <jk>while</jk> (<jv>r</jv>.canRead()) {
190    *          Item <jv>item</jv> = <jv>r</jv>.read(Item.<jk>class</jk>);
191    *          process(<jv>item</jv>);
192    *       }
193    *       <jv>r</jv>.next();  <jc>// consume the closing ]</jc>
194    *    }
195    * </p>
196    *
197    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the next operation can be {@code read}; <jk>false</jk> if the
198    *    cursor is positioned at a structural separator, field name, end-container, or end-of-stream.
199    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred peeking at the underlying stream.
200    * @throws ParseException If the input is malformed for the format.
201    */
202   default boolean canRead() throws IOException, ParseException {
203      return false;
204   }
205
206   /**
207    * Consumes the upcoming value (object, array, or scalar) and binds it to a POJO via the
208    * format's existing parser.
209    *
210    * <p>
211    * <b>Cursor positioning:</b> the cursor must be in a state where the next call to {@link #next()}
212    * would emit a value-shaped token (i.e. {@link TokenType#START_OBJECT}, {@link TokenType#START_ARRAY},
213    * or one of the {@code VALUE_*} tokens).  Calling {@code read} consumes that upcoming value
214    * wholesale and advances the cursor's depth back to where it was before the value would have
215    * started &mdash; structurally equivalent to calling {@link #next()} on a scalar, or
216    * {@link #next()} + {@link #skipChildren()} on a container.
217    *
218    * <p>
219    * <b>Do NOT call {@link #next()} before {@code read}.</b>  Once the structural opener
220    * (e.g. <c>{</c> or <c>[</c>) has been consumed by {@link #next()}, the underlying parser cannot
221    * re-bind the value.  The contract is "instead-of-next, not after-next."
222    *
223    * <p>
224    * <b>Default behavior:</b> the default implementation throws
225    * {@link UnsupportedOperationException}.  Format-specific token readers override this by
226    * delegating to their existing {@code *ParserSession} at the cursor's current input position
227    * and resyncing the cursor's state machine afterward.
228    *
229    * @param <T> The expected return type.
230    * @param type The target Java type.
231    * @return The bound POJO.
232    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
233    * @throws ParseException If the input is malformed for the format or cannot be bound to the type.
234    * @throws IllegalStateException If the cursor is not positioned at a value boundary.
235    * @throws UnsupportedOperationException If this format's reader has not implemented the bridge
236    *    (e.g. writer-only formats with no parser, or formats whose Phase-2 increment did not include
237    *    the bridge).
238    */
239   default <T> T read(Class<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException {
240      throw new UnsupportedOperationException(
241         "read is not implemented for " + getClass().getName());
242   }
243
244   /**
245    * Same as {@link #read(Class)} but accepts a pre-resolved {@link ClassMeta}.
246    *
247    * @param <T> The expected return type.
248    * @param type The target type.
249    * @return The bound POJO.
250    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
251    * @throws ParseException If the input is malformed or cannot be bound to the type.
252    */
253   default <T> T read(ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException {
254      throw new UnsupportedOperationException(
255         "read is not implemented for " + getClass().getName());
256   }
257
258   /**
259    * Same as {@link #read(Class)} but accepts a parameterized {@link Type} and optional type
260    * arguments (the same shape as {@link Parser#parse(Object, Type, Type...)}).
261    *
262    * @param <T> The expected return type.
263    * @param type The target type.
264    * @param args Optional type arguments for parameterized types.
265    * @return The bound POJO.
266    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
267    * @throws ParseException If the input is malformed or cannot be bound to the type.
268    */
269   default <T> T read(Type type, Type... args) throws IOException, ParseException {
270      throw new UnsupportedOperationException(
271         "read is not implemented for " + getClass().getName());
272   }
273
274   /**
275    * Skips over the children of the current structural token.
276    *
277    * <p>
278    * If the current token is {@link TokenType#START_OBJECT} or {@link TokenType#START_ARRAY},
279    * the cursor is advanced to the matching {@link TokenType#END_OBJECT} or
280    * {@link TokenType#END_ARRAY} (i.e. the entire sub-tree is consumed).  For any other token this
281    * is a no-op.
282    *
283    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
284    * @throws ParseException If the input is malformed for the format.
285    */
286   void skipChildren() throws IOException, ParseException;
287
288   /**
289    * Structural token cursors are always O(1)-memory streamers.
290    *
291    * @return Always <jk>true</jk>.
292    */
293   @Override
294   default boolean isStreaming() { return true; }
295
296   /**
297    * Closes the underlying stream.
298    *
299    * <p>
300    * Implementations should be idempotent &mdash; closing a closed reader is a no-op.
301    */
302   @Override
303   void close() throws IOException;
304
305   // =================================================================================
306   // Binary-native opt-in metadata accessors.  Default cursors and formats without
307   // native concepts inherit the safe defaults below: getNativeKind() returns NONE,
308   // getTagCount() returns 0, and the value accessors throw.  Format-specific cursors
309   // override the relevant subset (CBOR overrides getNativeKind/getTagCount/getTag/
310   // getSimpleValue; MsgPack overrides getNativeKind/getExtType).  See
311   // {@link BinaryNativeKind} for the catalog of native concepts.
312   // =================================================================================
313
314   /**
315    * Returns the binary-native classification of the current token.
316    *
317    * @return The native kind, or {@link BinaryNativeKind#NONE NONE} when the current token
318    *    carries no binary-native metadata.  Never {@code null}.
319    */
320   default BinaryNativeKind getNativeKind() {
321      return BinaryNativeKind.NONE;
322   }
323
324   /**
325    * Returns the number of semantic tags wrapping the current token.
326    *
327    * @return The tag count (0 when {@link #getNativeKind()} is not
328    *    {@link BinaryNativeKind#CBOR_TAG CBOR_TAG}).
329    */
330   default int getTagCount() {
331      return 0;
332   }
333
334   /**
335    * Returns the semantic tag at the specified position in the tag stack.
336    *
337    * @param index The tag position; outermost tag is {@code 0}.
338    * @return The tag number.
339    * @throws IndexOutOfBoundsException If {@code index} is outside {@code [0, getTagCount())}.
340    */
341   default long getTag(int index) {
342      throw new IndexOutOfBoundsException("Tag index " + index + " out of bounds for tagCount " + getTagCount());
343   }
344
345   /**
346    * Returns the typed-binary type byte for the current token.
347    *
348    * @return The signed type byte (range {@code -128..127}).
349    * @throws IllegalStateException If {@link #getNativeKind()} is not
350    *    {@link BinaryNativeKind#MSGPACK_EXT MSGPACK_EXT}.
351    */
352   default int getExtType() {
353      throw new IllegalStateException("Current token is not a typed-binary (ext) value (nativeKind=" + getNativeKind() + ")");
354   }
355
356   /**
357    * Returns the opaque simple value for the current token.
358    *
359    * @return The simple int.
360    * @throws IllegalStateException If {@link #getNativeKind()} is not
361    *    {@link BinaryNativeKind#CBOR_SIMPLE CBOR_SIMPLE}.
362    */
363   default int getSimpleValue() {
364      throw new IllegalStateException("Current token is not a simple value (nativeKind=" + getNativeKind() + ")");
365   }
366}
